inline_comments.message:
Yep, and NPE will be caught on line 102. Then getInput() returns null passed to listConfiguration(ConfigurationQuery query) and there NPE was not processed. Now it is.

May be it makes sense to add checking for null also in the getInput() method to skip execution of the rest of the code. 

Assert - my fault, will fix asap.

Sorry, but my real Q is "Why you changed this particular method to catch NPE, and did not change all others?"
Ex.
getConfigurationValue:60 (if queryData is NULL)
getHints:81 (if queryData is NULL)
etc.
I'm not suggesting to change them, "why specifically this one?"

Also, you added NPE-check for public method currently used ONLY in test, what is a point?

Well, I just followed warnings from sonar and this particular method is the first one in calls chain prone to NPE, but actually getHints() doesn't process NPE too and also has to be fixed. Others catch NPE. The current usage of listConfiguration() was out of my concerns.
UPD: if getHints:81 failed with NPE it will be caught by getConfigurationValue(ConfigurationQuery queryData) on L72, so it's safe to leave it as is.
